paper_map.title: Effects of Nitrogen on the Microstructure and Mechanical Properties of Fe–28Mn–10Al–0.8C Low-Density Steel
paper_map.abstract_summary: This study investigates the relationships among nitrogen content, inclusion characteristics, microstructure, and mechanical properties in Fe-28Mn-10Al-0.8C low-density steel. Nitrogen addition refines austenite grain size and enhances ultimate tensile strength, plasticity, fracture toughness, and impact toughness, but reduces yield strength due to decreased ferrite content. AlN is identified as the dominant inclusion in high-nitrogen steel, with 84% of AlN particles smaller than 2 μm contributing to grain refinement, while aggregated AlN inclusions promote crack initiation.
paper_map.research_objective: To elucidate the effects of nitrogen addition on the non-metallic inclusion characteristics, microstructure evolution, and resultant mechanical properties of Fe-28Mn-10Al-0.8C low-density steel.

key_sections.role: Background on low-density steels, nitrogen effects in steels, nitride inclusions, and research gap.
Alloy preparation with controlled nitrogen, hot forging, mechanical testing (tensile, impact), and microstructural/inclusion characterization (SEM, EDS, ImageJ).
Presentation of mechanical property comparison (YS, UTS, RA, A, CVN), microstructure (grain size, ferrite fraction, twins), and inclusion characteristics (size, number, distribution).

Mechanistic analysis of nitrogen effects on inclusion formation, grain refinement, ferrite suppression, strengthening/toughening mechanisms, fracture behavior, and role of aggregated vs dispersed AlN.
Summary of key findings linking nitrogen addition to inclusion population, microstructure refinement, and trade-offs in mechanical properties (strength increase, yield strength decrease).
